title: Pesto Rosso

overview.description: A rich Italian red pesto made from sun-dried tomatoes, Parmesan, basil and toasted nuts. Perfect stirred through pasta, spread on sandwiches or used as a flavour base.

method: Toast the pine nuts in a dry frying pan over a medium heat until lightly golden.
Place the sun-dried tomatoes, Parmesan, basil, garlic and pine nuts into a food processor.
Slowly add the olive oil while blending until a coarse but spoonable pesto forms.
Taste and season with black pepper.
Cover the surface with a thin layer of olive oil before refrigerating or freezing.

learning.did-you-know: Pesto Rosso originated in Sicily, where sun-dried tomatoes are abundant thanks to the island's warm Mediterranean climate.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance salty cheese, sweet tomatoes and rich olive oil into a versatile Italian sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A versatile freezer staple that can transform pasta, sandwiches and roasted vegetables in minutes.
chapman-tips: Pine nuts are traditional, but toasted cashews or blanched almonds make excellent everyday alternatives.
Don't over-blend—the pesto should retain a little texture.
Covering the surface with olive oil helps preserve its colour.
Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions for quick meals.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in silicone ice cube trays or small containers for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Stir into pasta, spread on homemade focaccia or use as a marinade for chicken.
